title: hieracium floribundum subsp. cochleatum
intro: Hieracium floribundum subsp. cochleatum, also known as hawkweed or cockleburr, is a flowering plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Hieracium floribundum subsp. cochleatum include reducing fever, alleviating arthritis symptoms, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
